core_mechanism: a convex/LP-duality optimality certificate that reduces optimality to a geometric cone-covering (Farkas-type) condition — the set of 'required' directions imposed by binding constraints must be fully contained in the cone of 'covered' directions generated by the problem's structure, with one-parameter thresholds marking transitions between qualitatively distinct optimal regimes
brief_reason: The abstract builds a duality-based framework in which the distribution pins down required directions of binding feasibility constraints and complementarity determines covered directions, and states that optimality reduces to full coverage of the required directions by the covered ones, with threshold values separating distinct optimal solution structures.
